guest: David Friedberg (Cana founder), Raoul Pal (former hedge fund manager, Real Vision), John/Ben (unclear, discussing Russian oligarchs)

predictions.text: Raoul Pal predicts crypto (especially Ethereum) will keep rising because network effects align owners and users.

dynamics: This is a 'Best of the Week' clip-show hosted by Producer Ben, not a standard Sam/Shaan episode. Three unrelated clips are stitched together: Friedberg on thinking big, Raoul Pal on crypto network effects, and Sam narrating a story about Russian oligarchs. No Sam/Shaan back-and-forth dynamic present.
frameworks: Dare to dream (TPB principle): set audacious goals even if the path isn't visible, to avoid boxing yourself into a smaller market.
Metcalfe's law applied to crypto: network value scales with connected users, explaining exponential price behavior.
learnings: Founders often self-limit by chasing the guaranteed near-term customer (e.g., restaurants) instead of the harder, bigger consumer market, permanently narrowing their ambition.
Crypto assets behave less like commodities (mean-reverting) and more like network businesses, so traditional supply/demand commodity logic doesn't apply to valuing them.
Unlike traditional network businesses where shareholders and users are separate, crypto lets users own equity in the network they use, intensifying the network effect.

Russia's oligarch class was created almost overnight through a rigged 1995 loans-for-shares auction where a handful of insiders acquired state oil and mineral giants for a tiny fraction of their eventual value.
Putin's wealth and power consolidation allegedly came from selectively jailing one oligarch as a warning to the rest, cementing loyalty rather than through transparent means.

banger_quotes.quote: Your team is only as good as you challenge them to be.
This is like behavioral economics, manna from heaven. Religion meets Metcalfe's law.
How does a 38 year old become worth $3 billion? What the hell?

numbers_dropped: Oligarchs paid ~$100M for a company that became one of the top 10 oil companies in the world
One oligarch's net worth reportedly reached $83 billion at peak
Company profits grew 50x and market caps grew 100x after the loans-for-shares deals
One jailed oligarch was worth ~$15 billion before his 14-year sentence
Putin's alleged private residence estimated at $1-2 billion
notable: This is a clip-compilation 'Best of the Week' episode narrated by Producer Ben rather than a standard Sam Parr/Shaan Puri episode, with no original business-idea brainstorming.
